title: Chapter 16
text: Chapter Thirteen L ara bit down on the cucumber to hold it in her mouth. Leaning forward, she placed her hands on the edge of Ronin’s work table and pushed it to the other side of the main room, directly before the wide front window. Climbing atop it, she drew her legs up and draped a forearm over her knees. The cucumber crunched when she took a bite. Its fresh, mild flavor — with just a hint of bitterness — burst over her tongue . She looked out the window as she ate, over the wall of shrubs that surrounded Ronin’s home and to the field across the street. Ronin called it a park . The sun was bright today, shining through the trees to cast dancing shadows over the lush grass beyond . In the distance, strangely shaped bots tended the park. Some of them looked a lot like the cars in the book upstairs. These seemed to be trimming the grass. Others — more humanlike — moved amongst the bushes and trees scattered around the grounds, clipping and watering . Lara envied the bots their purpose; damn it, she needed one, too! She’d never been idle for so long. A week in this house with nothing to do but stare out the windows was driving her crazy. It was a roof over her head, yeah, and food in her belly and clothes on her back, so she’d be stupid to screw it up…but boredom was harder to deal with than she’d imagined . When Ronin was around, it wasn’t nearly as bad. It was still hard to look at him, after the dream, but their conversations broke up the tedium of her days. While he was gone — whether resupplying at the Market or searching the Bot District for sign of Tabitha — she wandered around the house, as though she’d suddenly find something new in the mostly-empty rooms . If she were honest with herself, part of her was grateful when he was gone. Though nothing had been as vivid as that first dream, he remained in her thoughts at night, their bodies intertwining in the depths of her sleep. She saw flashes of that too-real dream whenever she looked at him . Weren’t dreams supposed to fade over time? This one had only intensified as days passed . This morning — not that midday could really be considered morning — she’d stepped out of her room to see his door closed. After her usual routine in the toilet room, he still hadn’t emerged, so she wandered downstairs to get something to eat . She’d danced for him each night, sometimes in the kitchen, sometimes here in the main room. Never the same moves, never quite the same rhythm or flow. Ronin, however, was unchanging. He sat and stared, expressionless and without comment. His answers to her questions remained infuriatingly brief and evasive. And, despite that, she still couldn’t look at him without thinking of his body atop hers, his hands all over her , his — No. Stop thinking about it ! She took another bite, chewing quickly in her annoyance. These thoughts had to stop . When she finished the cucumber, she wrapped her arms around her legs, locking them in place by grasping her wrist. She tapped her feet on the table and hummed softly to occupy her mind. Soon, she was swept up in the song; she swayed her shoulders and rocked side-to-side, hair brushing against her back . Something moved in her periphery. Lara jumped, releasing her legs. Her toes hit the wall, and she hissed at the pain . “Dammit, Ronin, can’t you ever say something when you walk into a room? Or do you always have to do it like some creep?” She glared at him, rubbing her sore toes . He stared back at her from the bottom step. “Am I required to announce myself in my own residence ?” “Yeah. You are. You don’t just sneak up on people like that .” He tilted his head, eyes narrowing. “Why are you sitting on the table ?” She glanced down, ran her palm over the table’s surface, and looked back to him. “ Why not ?” Ronin lifted a hand, extended a finger, and pointed to the chair she’d left near the other wall . “What about it?” She tossed her hair back over her shoulder . “That’s a piece of furniture designed specifically to be sat upon .” “ So ?” “That,” he continued, gesturing at the table, “ is not .” Pressing her palms to the table on either side of her, Lara bounced her ass atop it and wriggled her hips, settling down. “Works just fine for me. Benefit of being human, I guess. You’d break it if you sat on it .” “What does that have to do with it not being intended for sitting on ?” “Nothing. I can do whatever the hell I want .” His eyes widened, just a tiny bit, and he dropped his hand to his side. Time crept past. Distantly, the buzz of the bots working the park drifted into the silence . “Can you?” he finally asked . “ Can’t you ?” Ronin frowned. Had she gone too far? He hadn’t touched her yet, but she’d seen him moving his fingers as though he wanted to more than once. Bots did what they said, and he said he wouldn’t … But Ronin wasn’t like other bots . She forced that thought aside, before it led her back to the dream . “I can, but I limit my actions by considering the potential consequences. For example.” He crossed the room toward her, boots thumping on the floor — she knew the bastard snuck around on purpose. He stopped beside the table . This would be it. He’d grab hold of her and make her hate bots all over again, because of a fucking table. But that’s what she wanted, right? To have her hate back, to end her conflicting emotions ? “ What are — ” Before she could finish, he grasped the edge of the table with one hand and tipped it up. Lara’s stomach lurched as she was lifted. She pushed away from the table and, somehow, got her feet beneath her . She spun to face him. “The hell did you do that for ?” He eased the table back onto its legs. “Because I can do whatever the hell I want .” “Oh, you wanna play it that way , huh ?” She stalked across the room, fists balled at her sides, and stopped beside the chair. Turning, she met his gaze and kicked the chair over. The sound was enormous in the relative quiet . Ronin didn’t flinch, didn’t blink; he just stared . “Do you feel better now?” he asked, and she hated the genuine curiosity in his voice . “I’m bored .” “I would appreciate it if, in the future, you tell me such things sooner. I’d rather spare the furniture such abuse, when possible .” “I’m sure the chair will survive .” “How do we relieve your boredom ?” The image of his hand under her shirt, slowly sliding up to her breasts, blasted to the forefront of her mind. She forced it away as quickly as it came. “I don’t know. I just need something to do. I’m used to scavenging during the day, or helping the neighbors with repairs. Not just…sitting around !” “Is it more bearable if you stand ?” It took a moment, but when it hit her, she narrowed her eyes at him. “Smart ass. Why is it you only have a sense of humor sometimes ?” “Memory damage. Leaves me terribly inconsistent .” Lara smirked. “You can say that again. Not literally !” He snapped his mouth shut . “I just need something to do, Ronin. Something to keep me busy. Teach me something, anything. Just so I’m not counting the minutes .” “All right,” he said, looking toward the window. “Tables are for sitting at , not on . Have you learned something ?” “Yeah. Will I obey? Probably not. I’m serious, Ronin. I need something to do. I don’t…I don’t have a purpose here .” He raised a hand and scratched his cheek, eyes still on the window. “I’ll see what I can find .” “There’s nothing here. I’ve looked .” “I mean when I go out again. I’m leaving tomorrow. Into the Dust .” Lara straightened, brows rising. “I can go with you! We’ll look for Tabitha together .” “No,” he replied, a sharp edge in his voice that she hadn’t heard from him before. He settled his eyes on her. “It’s too dangerous , Lara .” “I’ve scavenged for most of my life. I know the dangers .” “You know nothing of the dangers out there. Have you ever been beyond the ruins of Cheyenne ?” She threw her arms out in frustration. “You can’t expect me to just stay here!” Pacing, she shook her head. “How long are you gonna be gone ?” “Depends on what I find. If I push it, maybe two or three weeks .” “ What ? Tell me you’re just testing out that sense of humor. For the record, that’s not funny. I am not gonna stay here for three weeks by myself with nothing to do !” He turned his body toward her. It was an eerie, inhuman movement; his head remained fixed in place while the rest of him shifted. “I’ll make sure there’s plenty of — ” “I’m not kidding!” Cold dread clawed up her spine. She’d be trapped. Alone. Surrounded by bots. “You can’t leave me here that long .” His brow furrowed. “What’s an acceptable timeframe for you?” he asked, after a long silence . “A day. Maybe two. Though I don’t understand why you can’t just take me along .” He shook his head. The motions stopped too abruptly at each side, accelerated too quickly, giving it an unnatural look. “It’s too dangerous. The first storm without shelter…” He lifted his hands to waist-level, dropped his gaze to them, and curled his fingers. “Three days. There’s little chance of me getting far enough to find anything of value in less time than that .” She stared at him, clenching her jaw. “ Damn you .” “I have to uphold my part of the bargain. That means I need to earn credits. I can only do that with scrap.” He stuffed his hands into his pockets . “So take me back home, and the deal can be done .” What response could she expect from that? What response did she want ? “No,” he said too quickly, unexpected finality in his tone . “Why? What do you — ” Someone — or something — knocked on the front door. Lara snapped her mouth shut. She and Ronin both turned their heads to look at it. When their eyes met again, he seemed to have regained his composure. He tilted his head to the side, gesturing for her to go into the kitchen . “All the more reason why you should take me,” she whispered, walking past him. In the kitchen, she pressed herself to the wall beside the entry way; out of sight, but not out of earshot . The locks clicked as he opened the door . “Good afternoon, citizen,” said a flat voice. “I am here to complete your regularly scheduled home appliance maintenance .” “Not a good time,” Ronin replied. The door creaked, but the sound cut off with a dull thunk . She could almost imagine the metal foot that had stopped it . Lara’s heart thumped, like distant, rolling thunder in her ears. What if it was a gearhead, here because they knew who she was? What if Warlord wasn’t done with her yet … “It is my o-o-obligation to inform you that it has been s-s-s-sixty-five days since your last appliance inspection,” the maintenance bot said, voice dropping to a deep slur as it stuttered. “Regular maintenance is integral to the continued f-f-f-functioning of your home appliances .” “Another time.” Was that edge back in Ronin’s tone ? “It is m-m-m-my obligation to — ” “Come back next month .” “N-n-next month, okay! P-p-p-please have a w-wonderful afternoon !” The door closed, and the locks clicked into place. Ronin was halfway to the kitchen when Lara, forcing her breathing to slow, stepped into the entryway. They looked at one another for a time. His expression wasn’t entirely blank, but she couldn’t read it . “Give me a few hours,” he said. “I’ll try to find you something to do while I’m gone .” “What if they come back?” Would she have to hide out in the attic, like the last human who’d lived here? The undisturbed dust suggested that the maintenance bots never went up there . “They won’t .” “How can you be sure? If not them, what about the gearheads ?” “There’s the attic, and a crawlspace hatch under the stairs. If you get frightened, hide. Keep the pistol with you .” “I wouldn’t be frightened if you’d just take me back to my shack!” she growled. “And what good is that pistol, anyway? Guns don’t seem to do shit to you.” She gestured toward his abdomen . “You didn’t see the holes in my casing,” he replied. “Aim for the optics, if you need to shoot. That’s the most vulnerable point on most bots .” “Fine!” She stomped toward the stairs. “I’m just some expendable human, anyway. So, if you find me dead when you get back, don’t say I didn’t tell you so .” His hand darted out, catching her upper arm before she reached the first step. His grip was firm, but not painful, his skin oddly warm. He spun her to face him . “If you feel as though you’re not safe here, consider this,” his gaze dipped to his hand, and her eyes followed it, “grounds to end our agreement. But we both know there’s nothing waiting for you back there. No one to go home to. No food or clean water. I’m not your Tabitha, but I’m doing all in my power to keep you safe .” With each of his words, her anger cooled. Sorrow swept into its place. Her eyes stung with gathering tears, and the burning intensified when she blinked them away . “Bastard.” Lara yanked her arm out of his hold and hurried upstairs. She made sure to slam her bedroom door behind her . A s per her usual evening routine, Lara showered — it was addicting, and she struggled to keep from doing it more than once a day — and walked downstairs. Ronin had already covered his part of the routine; he’d laid a variety of food out on the table for her to choose from . Normally, they talked as she ate, but conversation didn’t come easily tonight. It wasn’t her dream, or their earlier argument. Her mind kept going back to what she’d seen in the mirror. To what she saw now, when she looked at her hands . They were soft. Clean. Her skin had a healthy glow she’d never seen before, and her bones seemed a little less prominent. The changes were subtle, but she couldn’t help noticing them. She ate two, sometimes three meals a day, and there were no more hunger pains . Was Tabitha experiencing the same thing? Was that why she agreed to live with a bot? She deserved comfort, especially after all her years of caring for Lara . Guilt gnawed at Lara even now; no matter how hard she’d tried, it had never been enough. Scavenging, bartering, dancing…she never earned her share. How many times had she lied to Tabitha, claiming she wasn’t hungry, just so her sister had the extra portion? The one time Lara stepped up, broke out of her rut to provide for Tabitha … “You’re quiet tonight,” Ronin said, jarring Lara from her thoughts just before they took that dark turn. She looked up at him; he stared at the uneaten food laid before her . “Just thinking,” she replied, picking at a piece of meat. She took a small bite . “You normally verbalize your thoughts without restraint. Why is tonight different ?” Because she felt…subdued. Useless. Weak. And he’d hammered her reality home, earlier . Both of us know there is nothing waiting for you back there. No one to go home to . He’d been right; she had nothing. No one. Even Gary and Kate had turned her away. She wouldn’t be surprised if someone had already taken over her shack, if there was a stranger sleeping on her pallet, using the space she and Tabitha had built together . There was nothing to go back to . “Because I feel . And, right now, I don’t feel like talking .” “Are you implying that I don’t feel ?” “You’re smart. Figure it out .” He was quiet while she stared at her meal, chewing slowly and tasting little. What the hell would a bot know about any of it, anyway? They lived in luxury, with conveniences they didn’t even need, oblivious to the squalor of everyday human life . “We’ll find Tabitha,” he said after a while . Lara looked up at him with wide eyes. Was he…comforting her? She’d accused him of not feeling, but she knew it was wrong. How many times had he expressed some form of emotion to her? Anger, joy, curiosity, and now, concern . But how could bots feel? They were machines. Hunks of metal and parts. They didn’t have brains, or hearts, or any organs at all ! “You promise?” she asked, despite herself . “I…give you my word that I will do everything I can .” She sighed, dropping her gaze again. “Guess that’s all I can ask for .” The silence that settled between them was as big and imposing as the wall around the Bot District. Growing up, she’d never thought it was possible for a person to lose their appetite — how could you, when there was never enough to eat? — but suddenly the food in front of her lost its appeal . She wrapped the leftovers and stood, walking to the refrigerator to place them inside. Another wonder of life amongst bots; food could be stored in this big box, and it would keep fresh for days . “Guess we’ll get to it,” she said, glancing to him, “since you’re going to be gone for a few days and all .” Ronin’s face slackened, and it seemed he was about to say something. Maybe that it was all right, that she didn’t have to dance tonight because she was distressed and tired. Or that, since he’d touched her and broken his word, it was okay for her to break hers tonight . Instead, he pressed his lips into a tight line and nodded . So, Lara danced, as she had every night after her first in this house. Her movements were stiff, limbs heavy, and steps clumsy. She was numb, her mind elsewhere . To make it worse, Ronin sat motionless as he watched, conveying no enjoyment. He looked bored or disinterested, no different than the other times . She neared the table, spun, and slammed her fists atop it . “What the fuck is the point of this?” she screamed, breath ragged . His only movement was to angle his head up slightly, meeting her eyes. “We made an arrangement .” “Who cares about the arrangement? I want to know why ! You watch and watch, and for what? It’s like I’m dancing for a freaking wall !” “Because I want to understand . I watch you dance, and it fascinates me, because I’ve never seen anything like it. I know all the muscles and bones that enable your movement, understand the scientific laws that dictate your momentum and balance. There should be no mystery. But when you dance — when you truly dance — I can’t look away .” “You wanna know the big fucking secret?” She leaned over the table, closing the distance between them. “ It’s called being alive !” His eyebrows angled down, his eyes narrowed, and his jaw bulged, all so slightly that she might have imagined it. But the subtlety of his expression warned her. She backed away from the table . When Ronin moved, it was fast. His chair fell backward as he stood. With an almost casual sweep of his arm, he batted the table away, knocking it onto its side. The gap between them shrank with his relentless advance . Warlord’s scarred face entered her mind. This was close to the way he’d moved, that night . Lara scurried around the counter, desperate for something solid between them. For a terrifying moment, she was convinced he’d smash his way straight through it. She turned away, dropped into a crouch, and raised her arms to shield herself from the coming punishment . “Does a thing have to be flesh and blood to be alive?” he asked, voice low and brimming with anger. “Do humans own that term, that they get to define it ?” She risked a peek at him just as he slammed a fist on the countertop. The crack was loud enough to make her flinch. She stumbled back, a small, frightened sound escaping her throat. Hadn’t she learned anything? Couldn’t she just shut her mouth and dance? It didn’t matter if he enjoyed it; as long as he fed her, why should she care ? “I think, I reason, I react to the world around me. I question what I know and see, and I wonder what the future might bring, though I know it won’t likely be different than the past. I hope !” His voice dwindled, becoming something raw. “I yearn .” Slowly, Lara lowered her arms and looked up at him. His back was to her, hands near his waist with his head angled down toward them. The way he’d sounded just then…it was more human than she’d ever heard him. No matter how many times she tried to convince herself he was just another bot, she knew, at heart, he was different from the gearheads. Different from Warlord . Different from them all . It would have been easy for Ronin to use his fists on her, to batter her flesh and break her bones. Warlord had. But Ronin didn’t so much as touch her. No, it had been her who hurt him . “I’m sorry,” she whispered. Such simple words, with such deep impact. He’d used them when he thought he hurt her. How could she think he was unfeeling ? “I must depart tomorrow.” He turned his head toward her, but didn’t meet her gaze . Her stomach twisted, a heavy weight within dragging her insides down. Lara had caused this rift between them. She’d driven him away, when he had tried so hard to please her . “I…” He fell silent . Lara’s heart pounded, and she couldn’t get enough air in her lungs . “I don’t want to leave with this between us .” Everything stopped. His words hung in the air, and Lara couldn’t think. She stared at him, and he turned fully toward her . “The fault is mine. I thought your needs would be simple. That mine would be, too.” He shook his head, smoother this time than the last. “I’m learning. Forgive me, Lara Brooks .” Placing a hand on the counter, Lara pulled herself to her feet. Her fear was gone as quickly as it had come. Awe took its place. Learning. He was learning . “Only if you can forgive me, too,” she said, taking a hesitant step closer to him . “We both assumed understanding of one another, without considering what that really means.” He tilted his head down and ran a finger over the deep crack in the stone countertop. “I guess we really are made in the images of the Creators, if we share some of the same flaws .” Lara moved around the counter. “My experiences with bots don’t put them in a good light for me, Ronin. But the others I’ve dealt with wouldn’t have thought twice about doing that—” she pointed to the crack “— to me .” She stopped in front of him, looking up at his face. “And no bot’s ever apologized to me. For anything .” He shook his head, but his eyes ran over her body, engulfing her in their depth. “You’ve known so few of us. The ones you have as an example are…an anomaly .” “They were my reality. All the same…or worse .” “What can I do, Lara? To make you happy tonight? To see you smile before I leave ?” His words caught her by surprise, and, ridiculous as it felt, brought a smile to her lips. Tears burned in her eyes. She released a small, embarrassed laugh . “You’re turning me into such a sap,” she said, wiping her eyes with the back of her hand . He wanted her happy. Not just comfortable, not simply in good health to dance for him, but happy . No one besides Tabitha had ever cared about Lara’s feelings. No one until Ronin . He closed the short distance between them. Holding her gaze with his, he slowly raised a hand to her face and brushed the moisture from her cheek. His touch was so light, so gentle, that she couldn’t believe it was the same hand that shattered the counter . Lara stood totally still. The contact was foreign to her, yet it was…right. She understood that he’d stop if she asked, that she could pull away and he wouldn’t pursue her. Instead, she leaned into his touch, pressing her cheek to his palm. It was warm . Real . “I’ll come back as quickly as I can,” he said . “I’ll be waiting for you .”
